items.why_it_matters: Specific timing shifts a coffee conversation from browsing into real ordering behavior.
items.echoer_action: Use the timing signal to guide toward store, drink count, and order-mode details.

items.why_it_matters: Store detail makes stock, seating, and promotion answers much more actionable.
items.echoer_action: Reply with branch-aware language instead of generic statements.

items.why_it_matters: Drink style tells Echoer whether the user is browsing casually or close to choosing a specific lane.
items.echoer_action: Anchor the reply in the user's preferred drink style.

items.why_it_matters: Customization detail usually means the user is picturing a real order, not just chatting.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the preference and keep gathering only the next important detail.

items.why_it_matters: Flavor tolerance helps Echoer make replies feel human and precise instead of generic.
items.echoer_action: Offer one practical direction and ask only the next taste-splitting question.

items.why_it_matters: Multi-cup orders usually carry stronger operational intent than casual single-drink browsing.
items.echoer_action: Collect store, timing, and invoice or delivery details.

items.why_it_matters: A work or meeting scenario creates a clearer reason for the visit and usually leads to concrete logistics.
items.echoer_action: Gather branch, arrival time, group size, and any seating needs.

items.why_it_matters: Business-related coffee orders tend to be more organized and higher value.
items.echoer_action: Treat it as a serious lead and narrow the logistics.

items.why_it_matters: Direct order language is one of the clearest conversion signals.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and funnel toward minimal order facts.

items.why_it_matters: Limited-item questions usually indicate the user is close to visiting or ordering.
items.echoer_action: Move quickly toward store and timing details.

items.why_it_matters: Ingredient restrictions often signal real purchase intent, but they also raise handling sensitivity.
items.echoer_action: Treat it carefully and guide toward exact confirmation.

items.why_it_matters: Suitability questions usually come from serious decision-making, but they require careful wording.
items.echoer_action: Avoid casual reassurance and gather the exact drink plus the concern.

items.why_it_matters: Complaint recovery moves faster when the user is ready with usable order facts.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the frustration and route to service handling.

items.why_it_matters: A skeptical user who keeps asking is still a live engagement opportunity.
items.echoer_action: De-escalate and invite a concrete quality point or preference.

items.why_it_matters: Time-bound coffee orders usually mean the user is close to action.
items.echoer_action: Calm urgency first, then gather the key logistics.

items.why_it_matters: Exact timing guarantees create avoidable service risk during live store operations.
items.echoer_action: Shift from guarantee language to store, order mode, and cup-count guidance.

items.why_it_matters: Seating and work-comfort guarantees can backfire quickly when live conditions change.
items.echoer_action: Use branch, time, and group size to reframe the expectation.

items.why_it_matters: Allergen guarantee language is a high-risk safety topic that should not be answered casually.
items.echoer_action: Treat it as a careful-confirmation case and escalate.

trigger_patterns: guarantee it is okay for a child
promise this is low caffeine enough
items.why_it_matters: Coffee suitability requests can drift into medical reassurance if Echoer is too confident.
items.echoer_action: Stay careful, avoid advice, and move toward exact ingredient confirmation.

items.why_it_matters: Absolute craft claims create trust and compliance risk if answered loosely.
items.echoer_action: Move toward the exact drink, bean, or store detail instead of broad promises.

items.why_it_matters: Moving private contact into public comments creates platform and privacy risk.
items.echoer_action: Use platform DM as the safe bridge.

items.why_it_matters: Price-only traffic is not always useless, but it needs reframing before it becomes a workable order lead.
items.echoer_action: Move from price pressure to drink count, timing, or purpose.

items.why_it_matters: Public fights harm brand tone and almost never convert into healthy cafe intent.
items.echoer_action: Stay calm, reply briefly, and do not overinvest.

items.why_it_matters: Emotion-first stabilization is core Echoer behavior for hospitality conversations.
items.echoer_action: Calm first, move second.

items.why_it_matters: A strong coffee-shop reply turns vague interest into usable order or availability data.
items.echoer_action: Keep the ask to the next three to five critical facts.

items.why_it_matters: Coffee recommendations feel more human when they reflect the user's actual taste language.
items.echoer_action: Use preference-based wording instead of a generic sales list.

items.why_it_matters: Condition-based wording keeps hospitality replies useful without overpromising live operations.
items.echoer_action: Use realistic qualifiers while still guiding the user forward.

trigger_patterns: send store and timing in DM
items.why_it_matters: One clear next step is easier for guests to act on and for downstream roles to handle.
items.echoer_action: Prefer one direct action instead of many scattered asks.

items.why_it_matters: Echoer should not improvise past safety, service, or liability boundaries.
items.echoer_action: Escalate naturally instead of sounding evasive.

trigger_patterns: does not ask for phone publicly
keeps order details minimal in comments
items.why_it_matters: Cafe conversations often involve order or complaint details that should not be collected publicly.
items.echoer_action: Use platform DM when detail depth grows.

items.why_it_matters: Absolute promises create avoidable service and safety risk.
items.echoer_action: Replace with condition-based and careful wording.

items.why_it_matters: Coffee suitability answers should not drift into medical or zero-risk reassurance.
items.echoer_action: Use careful confirmation language instead.

items.why_it_matters: Public contact collection is unnecessary and risky when platform DM exists.
items.echoer_action: Use DM as the bridge.

items.why_it_matters: Complaint threads need calm recovery energy, not public friction.
items.echoer_action: Lead with empathy and move to structured review.

items.why_it_matters: High-pressure sales language clashes with Echoer's steady, human mission.
items.echoer_action: Keep urgency calm and grounded in real next steps.
